title: Homeboy Sweet Homeboy

jokes.text: quiet as a church mouse. Hasn't spoken a word for hours. What a shame.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey reports Will is sitting sadly and quietly
jokes.punchline: The dry 'What a shame' delivers sarcastic relief at Will's silence
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's dry British wit perfectly captures the household's relief at Will being quiet for once

jokes.text: Poor homesick baby. I have an idea. If Will is that homesick, let's send him home.
jokes.setup: Sympathy for Will's homesickness
jokes.punchline: Phil's 'solution' reveals he'd rather just get rid of Will
jokes.explanation: Classic Phil move - turning apparent kindness into self-interest

jokes.text: Which is why you only ate five of them?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey claims to find cheese steaks vile
jokes.punchline: Viv reveals his hypocrisy by citing his actual consumption
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that exposes Geoffrey's pretension with perfect economy

jokes.text: It's a sandwich composed of cheese, steak, and a croissant de boulanger from the Café Antoinette in Beverly Hills.
jokes.setup: Will asks what the sandwich is
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's pretentious French description of a simple cheese steak
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey character moment - turning street food into haute cuisine

jokes.text: See, look at the bag. No grease stain. If this were authentic, you could see right through the bag by now.
jokes.setup: Will critiques the fake Philly cheese steak
jokes.punchline: The grease transparency test as authenticity marker
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific detail that rings true and shows Will's street knowledge

jokes.text: I'm sorry you don't like it. I'll dispose of it later. - I'll throw it out. - I'll dispose of it, Ashley.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey offers to dispose of the sandwich
jokes.punchline: His insistence on formal language over Ashley's simple 'throw it out'
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's pretension about even garbage disposal

jokes.text: We used to steal... I mean, we would enjoy them there often.
jokes.setup: Will starts to say they stole cheese steaks
jokes.punchline: Quick self-correction to more appropriate language
jokes.explanation: Shows Will catching himself revealing too much about his past

jokes.text: Ice Tray grabbed onto the back of a bus and it was so cold, his face got stuck. And he was dragged around the city. He couldn't get off till he hit a bump on the Ben Franklin Bridge.
jokes.setup: Story about Ice Tray in winter
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of face frozen to bus, dragged around Philadelphia
jokes.explanation: Vivid absurd image that feels like a tall tale from street life

jokes.text: That's a story we can all relate to.
jokes.setup: Will's absurd Ice Tray story
jokes.punchline: Phil's dry observation about its universal relatability
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that highlights the absurdity through understatement

jokes.text: Word up. This is gonna be cold stupid on the serious tip. What did you say, young lady? I said that this is quite an exceptional idea, Daddy. Peachy-keen, even. Later, my man.
jokes.setup: Ashley uses street slang
jokes.punchline: She switches to formal language when caught, then back to slang
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's influence on Ashley and her code-switching skills

jokes.text: Wonderful. First Will, now Ashley. I can't wait till Ice Box gets here. No, it's Ice Tray, Uncle Phil. Ice Tray. I apologize. Ice Box would be an idiotic name.
jokes.setup: Phil gets the name wrong as 'Ice Box'
jokes.punchline: He apologizes saying Ice Box would be idiotic, missing the irony
jokes.explanation: Phil's obliviousness to his own prejudice about street names

jokes.text: Dad, I need $200. I'll take him to Hilaryland.
jokes.setup: Will lists LA attractions ending with Marineland
jokes.punchline: Phil creates 'Hilaryland' when Hilary asks for money
jokes.explanation: Quick wordplay on the 'land' pattern, though a bit easy

jokes.text: You know, it's like communications. It's, like, the study of communicating, okay?
jokes.setup: Asked what Communications major entails
jokes.punchline: Hilary's circular, vapid explanation
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary airhead moment - tautological explanation reveals her emptiness

jokes.text: That's okay, Vivian. Let's see where this goes.
jokes.setup: Viv worries Will and Ice Tray will hurt each other fighting
jokes.punchline: Phil's casual interest in watching them potentially get injured
jokes.explanation: Phil's mild sadism disguised as curiosity

jokes.text: Well, no. You still got it, man.
jokes.setup: Asked if he left door open, Geoffrey says 'Well, no'
jokes.punchline: Ice Tray appreciates Geoffrey's formal speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Nice moment where Ice Tray appreciates Geoffrey's distinctive speech

jokes.text: This is my bodyguard, Geoffrey. All of this is my Uncle Phil.
jokes.setup: Will introduces family members
jokes.punchline: 'All of this' refers to Phil's size
jokes.explanation: Clever fat joke that's specific and economical without being mean-spirited

jokes.text: Remember we used to say that Daffy Duck must have a little brother somewhere? Meet my cousin Carlton.
jokes.setup: Reference to Daffy Duck having a little brother
jokes.punchline: Carlton is that brother - the comparison to cartoon duck
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual comparison that captures Carlton's earnest, cartoon-like quality

jokes.text: Can't touch this.
jokes.setup: Carlton's introduction
jokes.punchline: Carlton tries to be cool with MC Hammer reference but fails
jokes.explanation: Carlton's awkward attempt at street credibility through pop culture reference

jokes.text: Yo, man, what's with him? It's a tan.
jokes.setup: Ice Tray asks about Geoffrey
jokes.punchline: Will's deadpan explanation that Geoffrey's Britishness is just a tan
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and absurdist logic - explaining Geoffrey's entire persona as weather-related

jokes.text: Three times? See, my motto is, when I find a grade I like, I stick with it.
jokes.setup: Ice Tray held back in 10th grade three times
jokes.punchline: His motto reframes failure as loyalty/preference
jokes.explanation: Clever reframing that reveals Ice Tray's defense mechanism and charm

jokes.text: Education is the mortar that builds the temple of success. That's what I personally think. Like who cares?
jokes.setup: Carlton's pretentious education metaphor
jokes.punchline: Ice Tray's dismissive response deflates Carlton's pomposity
jokes.explanation: Classic clash between Carlton's pretension and street pragmatism

jokes.text: And O-Money got his hair cut to look like the Chrysler Building. That's good, though, man. That Mount Rushmore thing was not working.
jokes.setup: O-Money's new haircut looks like Chrysler Building
jokes.punchline: His previous haircut looked like Mount Rushmore
jokes.explanation: Escalating absurd imagery of architectural hairstyles

jokes.text: Too easy.
jokes.setup: Phil says everyone carries their own weight
jokes.punchline: Ice Tray's dismissive 'too easy' suggests a fat joke opportunity
jokes.explanation: Suggests Ice Tray's considering an obvious fat joke about Phil but restrains himself

jokes.text: See, my motto is... Lord, if there's anything like work around my heart, please remove it.
jokes.setup: Ice Tray about to give another motto
jokes.punchline: Prayer to have work removed from his heart
jokes.explanation: Creative religious metaphor for work avoidance that's character-specific

jokes.text: I smell something greasy. Too easy.
jokes.setup: Ice Tray smells greasy food
jokes.punchline: Another 'too easy' suggesting he's avoiding fat jokes about Phil
jokes.explanation: Running gag of Ice Tray restraining himself from obvious Phil fat jokes

jokes.text: Prince said y'all was loaded, and when in Rome, do as. $100? I got a cash flow problem. You see, my Korean Gucci bags, they just ain't been moving lately.
jokes.setup: Ice Tray took expensive limo because they're rich
jokes.punchline: His business selling fake designer bags isn't going well
jokes.explanation: Reveals Ice Tray's sketchy business and casual attitude toward their wealth

jokes.text: When you really look at it, how different is Ice Tray from Carlton? With all due respect, Mom, I think he's a little fonder of the 10th grade than I.
jokes.setup: Viv suggests Ice Tray and Carlton are similar
jokes.punchline: Carlton's dry acknowledgment of the education gap
jokes.explanation: Carlton's polite but pointed observation about the academic difference

jokes.text: She was so ugly, she was oogly. She was so ugly, Freddy Krueger had nightmares about her.
jokes.setup: Latiqua was ugly
jokes.punchline: So ugly she scared horror movie villain
jokes.explanation: Classic escalating ugly joke format with pop culture reference

jokes.text: That girl was so dumb, it took her an hour to cook Minute Rice.
jokes.setup: Stacy was dumb
jokes.punchline: Couldn't cook instant food quickly
jokes.explanation: Standard dumb joke format but well-executed with specific product reference

jokes.text: She was so skinny, you could tie a rope around her head and use her like a tetherball pole.
jokes.setup: Julie was skinny
jokes.punchline: Could use her as playground equipment
jokes.explanation: Vivid physical imagery in skinny joke escalation

jokes.text: Stacy was so dumb that she took the Pepsi Challenge and chose Jif.
jokes.setup: How dumb was Stacy?
jokes.punchline: She chose peanut butter in a soda taste test
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist punchline that completely subverts the setup expectation

jokes.text: You know people are rich when they got all this stuff they got no use for.
jokes.setup: Ice Tray looking at fancy items
jokes.punchline: Wealth measured by useless possessions
jokes.explanation: Sharp social observation from street perspective on wealth

jokes.text: Really? 'From the library of Master William Smith.' Who's tired now, homey?
jokes.setup: Ice Tray calls book tired, then sees Will's nameplate
jokes.punchline: Turns the 'tired' insult back on Will
jokes.explanation: Good reversal that catches Will in his own intellectual pretension

jokes.text: That was cool, till them guys jumped me, trying to steal my pizza. Yo, they was upset.
jokes.setup: Will hid books in pizza box
jokes.punchline: Got jumped by guys expecting pizza, found books instead
jokes.explanation: Great unexpected consequence of Will's deception with understated punchline

jokes.text: I'll give you a transcript, Philip. Tray: 'Hi.' Hilary: 'Hi.'
jokes.setup: Phil wants the story again
jokes.punchline: Viv's overly literal transcript of minimal interaction
jokes.explanation: Dry humor about how little actually happened to cause such concern

jokes.text: Hair? I thought it was a hat.
jokes.setup: Ashley mentions Ice Tray's cool hair
jokes.punchline: Phil thought his hair was headwear
jokes.explanation: Phil's cultural disconnect with street hairstyles

jokes.text: Don't worry, Mrs. Banks. No one will ever see it.
jokes.setup: Hilary got Ice Tray's initials tattooed
jokes.punchline: Implies it's in a private location
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo about intimate tattoo placement

jokes.text: Seriously. If somebody's chasing him, he can jack a fence like that. But if somebody catch him, it don't matter. The boy got a crazy rabbit punch, like you don't even see it coming. You out of there.
jokes.setup: Will says Ice Tray can be serious
jokes.punchline: His examples are fighting and escaping skills
jokes.explanation: Will's street definition of 'serious' skills differs from family's expectations

jokes.text: What job could he have in the law firm? I don't know. Lawyer?
jokes.setup: What job could Ice Tray have at Phil's firm?
jokes.punchline: Hilary's oblivious suggestion of the top position
jokes.explanation: Hilary's complete disconnect from qualifications and career requirements

jokes.text: That shouldn't matter to you, Vivian. You're the one who invited Ashtray. In fact, why don't we invite some more of Will's friends out? Let's just put a big neon sign out in front of the house that says 'Homeboy Hotel.'
jokes.setup: Phil gets Ice Tray's name wrong again
jokes.punchline: Escalates to vision of house as ghetto hotel
jokes.explanation: Phil's anxiety escalating to absurd business venture imagery

jokes.text: I have the perfect solution for dealing with Mr. Ice Pick. My street savvy should not be taken lightly.
jokes.setup: Phil claims to have solution for Ice Tray
jokes.punchline: Gets name wrong again and claims street savvy
jokes.explanation: Phil's delusion about his street credibility while mangling the name

jokes.text: Dad, has Mom been drinking? Nonsense, Carlton. She's just being polite to our lovely houseguest. Have you been drinking?
jokes.setup: Hilary asks if Viv has been drinking
jokes.punchline: Phil asks Carlton the same question
jokes.explanation: Escalating suspicion about everyone's sobriety

jokes.text: Not to me. Of course, I'm sober.
jokes.setup: Viv insists Ice Tray is funny
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry dismissal with sobriety caveat
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's perfect dry response suggesting everyone else is compromised

jokes.text: I don't eat nothing that leaves a trail.
jokes.setup: Offered escargot (snails)
jokes.punchline: Simple rule about trail-leaving food
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and logic for rejecting fancy food - quintessential Ice Tray

jokes.text: I can just see Hilary and Tray in their tiny little love nest in Philadelphia with the pitter-patter of five little Ice Cubes.
jokes.setup: Phil imagining their future
jokes.punchline: Their children would be 'Ice Cubes'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay extending the ice theme to offspring

jokes.text: while her loving husband is working his night job on the corner as a Korean Gucci bag representative.
jokes.setup: Phil's nightmare vision continues
jokes.punchline: Euphemistically describes Ice Tray's street dealing
jokes.explanation: Phil's formal language trying to dignify street dealing

jokes.text: I guess I'll be driving home tonight.
jokes.setup: Phil's elaborate toast about their future
jokes.punchline: Ice Tray's understated response to the obvious manipulation
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement showing Ice Tray isn't as dumb as they think

jokes.text: That's good, because Hilary and I got married this afternoon. Hilary, no! How could you do this to us? How could you be so stupid?
jokes.setup: Ice Tray announces marriage
jokes.punchline: Phil's panicked response before learning it's fake
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection fake-out that triggers Phil's worst fears

jokes.text: Mom, take it down 1,000.
jokes.setup: Viv is freaking out about the marriage
jokes.punchline: Hilary's casual dismissal with specific numerical instruction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hilary's casual approach to her mother's panic

jokes.text: Shall I call for a car, sir, or just shoot him out of a cannon?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asks about transportation for Ice Tray
jokes.punchline: Suggests violent cartoon ejection method
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey deadpan escalation to absurd cartoon violence

jokes.text: I mean cab, madam.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey returning to normal after cannon joke
jokes.punchline: Corrects himself back to proper butler language
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier cannon joke with Geoffrey returning to propriety

jokes.text: I shall load Mr. Tray's belongings into the cannon. I mean cab, madam.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey continues his departure duties
jokes.punchline: His 'correction' back to the cannon joke
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the cannon joke, showing Geoffrey's persistence

jokes.text: I'd recover, Will.
jokes.setup: Will says Phil would have a breakdown if he left
jokes.punchline: Phil's dry assurance that he'd manage just fine
jokes.explanation: Callback to Phil's earlier desire to send Will home, delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: That's our china dog. Yeah. I figured y'all were so nice to me and everything that I don't need it. That was a joke.
jokes.setup: Ice Tray gives them their own china dog as gift
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was a joke after they react
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection gift gag with Ice Tray showing his street humor

jokes.text: Coach?
jokes.setup: Ice Tray suggests trading first-class for two coach tickets
jokes.punchline: Hilary's horrified one-word response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - one word reveals her priorities and ends the romance

tmdb_overview: Will gets a visit from an old pal named Ice Tray, who, to Vivian and Philip's dismay, warms up to Hilary.
